text: The whole world had one language and a common vocabulary.
As people moved eastward, they found a plain in Shinar and settled there.
They said to each other, let's make bricks and bake them thoroughly, using brick for stone and tar for mortar.
They said, let's build a city and a tower reaching the heavens, to make a name for ourselves so we won't be scattered over the earth.
The LORD came down to see the city and tower the people were building.
The LORD said that as one people with one language, nothing they planned would be impossible for them.
He said, let us go down and confuse their language, so they can't understand each other.
So the LORD scattered them from there over the whole earth, and they stopped building the city.
That is why it was called Babel, because there the LORD confused the world's language and scattered the people.
This is Shem's family line: two years after the flood, at age 100, Shem fathered Arphaxad.
After Arphaxad, Shem lived 500 more years and had other sons and daughters.
Arphaxad lived 35 years and fathered Shelah.
After Shelah, Arphaxad lived 403 more years and had other sons and daughters.
Shelah lived 30 years and fathered Eber.
After Eber, Shelah lived 403 more years and had other sons and daughters.
Eber lived 34 years and fathered Peleg.
After Peleg, Eber lived 430 more years and had other sons and daughters.
Peleg lived 30 years and fathered Reu.
After Reu, Peleg lived 209 more years and had other sons and daughters.
Reu lived 32 years and fathered Serug.
After Serug, Reu lived 207 more years and had other sons and daughters.
Serug lived 30 years and fathered Nahor.
After Nahor, Serug lived 200 more years and had other sons and daughters.
Nahor lived 29 years and fathered Terah.
After Terah, Nahor lived 119 more years and had other sons and daughters.
After Terah turned 70, he fathered Abram, Nahor, and Haran.
This is Terah's family line: Terah fathered Abram, Nahor, and Haran, and Haran fathered Lot.
Haran died in Ur of the Chaldeans, in his father Terah's presence.
Abram married Sarai, and Nahor married Milkah, daughter of Haran, who was also the father of Iscah.
Sarai was childless, having no children.
Terah took his son Abram, grandson Lot, and daughter-in-law Sarai and left Ur of the Chaldeans for Canaan, but they settled in Haran instead.
Terah lived 205 years and died in Haran.
